items.title: 'Better for victims': $18.9 million invested in director of public prosecutions

children.content: The funding will go towards 24 new, permanent positions within the office to meet its increasing case load, Attorney-General Tara Cheyne said.

items.title: Woman dies as fire in north Canberra unit causes 'substantial damage'

children.content: Emergency services were called to the Wallcliffe Flats in Belconnen at about 6:30am where they found a ground-floor unit alight.

image.altText: A fire truck and personnel.

items.title: 'How do I survive this?' The financial burden of Catalina's cancer treatment

children.content: Research from the Cancer Council shows more than 38 per cent of cancer patients lose their income during treatment. For Catalina Alcazar, it was an unexpected extra burden as she faced treatment for a tumour in her oesophagus.

gallery.altText: A woman smiles while standing in a park.

thumbnail.altText: A woman smiles while standing in a park.

items.title: Woman killed in bus crash asked to be organ donor just days earlier

children.content: Uqasha Imran was fatally injured when a bus driver struck her on her motorcycle in 2025. Now, her family are promoting the importance of organ donation while helping other young athletes like her.

image.altText: A man with short black hair sits at a table looking sad while holding an image of a young woman.

items.title: Man faces court charged with murdering elderly mother in her Canberra home

children.content: Marcus Kauffman made a brief appearance in the ACT Magistrates Court this morning, where no bail application was made.

image.altText: A crime scene at a residential home in O'Connor.

items.title: A Canberra woman's husband said not to book return flights. It was a case of exit trafficking

children.content: Advocates for migrant women say they're seeing more cases of exit trafficking — where an offender uses coercion, threats or deception to organise a person's departure from Australia.

items.title: Stranded driver, students among 10 rescues during heavy rainfall in NSW and Qld

children.content: Almost 50 high school students and teachers have been rescued after becoming stranded by floodwater at a national park in the Scenic Rim while on a school camp.
Earlier, near the New South Wales and Queensland border, crews worked to rescue a person stuck in a vehicle at Numinbah Valley near Murwillumbah.
NSW SES volunteers and emergency service partners have responded to 197 incidents since Monday afternoon.

children.content: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese announces the federal government has secured 600,000 barrels of jet fuel from China. Follow live.

items.title: Xi to welcome Putin for visit days after hosting Trump

children.content: While this meeting comes just days after Beijing hosted the US president, the Kremlin insists there is "no correlation" between the visits.

items.title: Man accused of killing wife and two children in Sydney home fronts court

items.title: What we know about the deadly shooting at a San Diego mosque

children.content: Two teenagers opened fire at the largest mosque in San Diego, California on Monday, killing three men. Here is what we know about the attack.

items.title: The dead fish waste disaster that Tasmania failed to plan for

children.content: High-volume salmon mortalities last year swamped Tasmania's waste streams as companies scrambled to find somewhere to accept them, documents show. There are now concerns the state remains unprepared for future mass deaths.

items.title: Farm worker given $5,000 cash to perform fatal amputation, court hears

children.content: Kalman Tal, 66, died soon after he asked for his left foot to be cut off in a public park with a battery-powered saw, a court has heard, but the man who performed the amputation has pleaded not guilty to murder.
